name: Scallops in Champagne Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 cup Champagne ("Brut", or "Extra Dry") or dry white wine
4 tbsp (60 ml) low-fat or non-fat sour cream
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the Champagne and shallots in a large saucepan that is large enough to hold the scallops. Bring to a boil over high heat.
2. Reduce the heat to medium and simmer until the liquid amount is reduced by half.
3. Add the scallops and continue cooking over medium heat for 5 to 7 minutes, until the contents of the pan almost reach a boil and the scallops have become firm and white. The scallops should be under-done at this point.
4. Remove the scallops with a slotted spoon and place in an oven-proof gratin dish, quiche pan, or other shallow container.
5. Thoroughly mix the flour with the sour cream.
6. Add the mixture to the liquid remaining in the pan, stirring to combine thoroughly.
7. Bring the liquid to a boil over high heat until it is the consistency of heavy cream. This may take from 2 to 5 minutes, depending on how much liquid the scallops gave up.
8. Spoon the liquid over the scallops and sprinkle with the bread crumbs.
9. Place under a pre-heated broiler until the bread crumbs have browned, 2 to 4 minutes.
10. Serve immediately. Serves 4 to 6, or 8 as an appetizer.
